lion.habitat: Grasslands and savannas of Africa and some parts of Asia
lion.diet: Carnivore – mainly antelopes, zebras, and buffalo
lion.behavior: Lives in prides, social hunting, territorial
lion.fun_fact: Called the 'king of the jungle' because of its dominance and strength

elephant.habitat: Forests, grasslands, and savannas in Africa and Asia
elephant.diet: Herbivore – eats grass, leaves, fruits, bark
elephant.behavior: Lives in herds led by a matriarch, intelligent and social
elephant.fun_fact: Elephants have strong memories and communicate using infrasound

dolphin.habitat: Oceans worldwide, usually in shallow coastal waters
dolphin.fun_fact: Can recognize themselves in mirrors, showing high cognitive ability

penguin.fun_fact: Penguins huddle together to keep warm in extreme cold

tiger.fun_fact: Each tiger has a unique stripe pattern

kangaroo.behavior: Live in mobs, hop using strong legs

panda.behavior: Solitary, mostly inactive during the day
panda.fun_fact: Pandas have a special thumb for holding bamboo

giraffe.behavior: Social, live in loosely organized herds
giraffe.fun_fact: Tallest land animal, neck can be up to 6 feet long

polar bear.fun_fact: Their skin is black under thick white fur to absorb heat

wolf.habitat: Forests, tundra, mountains of North America, Europe, Asia
wolf.fun_fact: Wolves communicate using howls, body language, and scent marking

eagle.habitat: Mountains, forests, and open areas worldwide
eagle.behavior: Strong fliers, build large nests called eyries

crocodile.habitat: Rivers, lakes, wetlands of Africa, Asia, Americas, Australia
crocodile.behavior: Ambush hunters, spend time in water and sunbathe
crocodile.fun_fact: Crocodiles can go months without eating after a big meal

zebra.fun_fact: Each zebra's stripe pattern is unique

gorilla.behavior: Live in troops led by a silverback male
gorilla.fun_fact: Gorillas use tools and have complex social behavior

whale.diet: Carnivore – krill, small fish (varies by species)
whale.behavior: Highly social, migratory, communicate with songs
whale.fun_fact: Blue whales are the largest animals ever known to live on Earth

chimpanzee.behavior: Live in communities, very intelligent, use tools
chimpanzee.fun_fact: Closely related to humans, sharing about 98% of DNA

cheetah.behavior: Fastest land animal, solitary or small groups
cheetah.fun_fact: Can reach speeds of 70 mph in short bursts

owl.fun_fact: Owls can rotate their heads up to 270 degrees

rhinoceros.habitat: Grasslands and forests of Africa and Asia
rhinoceros.conservation: Critically Endangered to Vulnerable depending on species
rhinoceros.fun_fact: Their horn is made of keratin, the same material as human hair

hippopotamus.habitat: Rivers, lakes, and swamps of Africa
hippopotamus.fun_fact: Hippos can hold their breath underwater for about 5 minutes
